post.description: Sends a one-time password to the provided email address
Verifies the OTP and creates a session

get.description: Returns the current user session if authenticated

post.description: Ends the current user session

patch.description: Updates the current authenticated user's information

get.description: Handles OAuth provider callbacks (redirects)

post.description: Creates a new experiment with the provided configuration

embargoUntil.description: Embargo end date and time (ISO datetime string, will be stored as UTC in database)

members.description: Optional array of member objects with userId and role

locations.description: Optional array of locations associated with the experiment

workbookId.description: Optional workbook ID to associate with the experiment

get.description: Returns a list of experiments based on the specified filter criteria, including search.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter experiments by relationship to the user

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific experiment

patch.description: Updates an existing experiment with the provided changes

embargoUntil.description: Updated embargo end date and time (ISO datetime string, will be stored as UTC in database)

anonymizeContributors.description: When true, the API rewrites contributor name/avatar/id to deterministic pseudonyms before responding

locations.description: Updated locations associated with the experiment

delete.description: Deletes an experiment and all associated data

get.description: Returns experiment details along with user access and admin status
get.summary: Get experiment details with access information

get.description: Returns a list of all users who are members of the specified experiment

post.description: Adds multiple members to the experiment with specified roles

userId.description: ID of the user to add as a member

role.description: Role to assign to the new member

delete.description: Removes a member from the experiment

patch.description: Updates the role of an existing experiment member

role.description: New role to assign to the member

post.description: Promotes (or adds) the given users as admins across multiple experiments the caller administers. Used to clear account-deletion blockers in one step.

experimentId.description: Experiment to transfer admin rights on

targetUserId.description: User to make an admin of the experiment

post.description: Submits a request from the signed-in user to join the experiment as a member. Returns the existing pending request if one already exists.

message.description: Optional short message to the project admins

get.description: Returns all pending join requests for the experiment.
get.summary: List pending join requests for an experiment

get.description: Returns the current user's pending join request, if any.
get.summary: Get the signed-in user's pending join request for an experiment

post.description: Admin-only. Approves a pending join request and adds the requester as a member.
Admin-only. Marks a pending join request as rejected.

delete.description: Cancels a pending join request submitted by the signed-in user.

get.description: Retrieves rows from an experiment table. Supports plain page-based reads, column projection, and structured filters/aggregation (JSON-encoded) using the same primitives as the persisted visualization config. When `aggregation` is set, page/pageSize are ignored and `limit` caps the result.

parameters.name: tableName
parameters.description: Table name: 'raw_data', 'device', macro UUID, or upload_table_id

parameters.name: columns
parameters.description: Specific columns to fetch. If provided with tableName, fetches full data for these columns only

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: Column name to order results by

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of filter conditions applied as a WHERE clause

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Hard cap on returned rows for filtered/aggregated reads. Ignored when page/pageSize are used.

name.description: Technical name of the table used for queries and operations

get.description: Returns the distinct (non-null) values of a single column on an experiment table, capped at `limit`. Powers the searchable categorical filter combobox and any consumer that needs a values list.
get.summary: Get distinct values for a column

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column whose distinct values to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Hard cap on returned values (default 200, max 1000).

truncated.description: True when the column has more values than `limit` returned

get.description: Retrieves metadata for all tables in the experiment (names, display names, row counts)

identifier.description: Stable identifier: static table name, macro UUID, or upload table name

tableType.description: Whether this is a static table, a macro table, or a user-uploaded table

displayName.description: Human-readable display name of the table for UI

totalRows.description: Total number of rows in the table

defaultSortColumn.description: Default column to sort by in the UI

errorColumn.description: Column name that contains error information if any

get.description: Returns the flow graph for the specified experiment.

post.description: Creates a new flow for the experiment with the provided graph.

put.description: Updates the existing flow for the experiment with the provided graph.

post.description: Attaches a workbook to the experiment, publishing a new version if cells have changed since the last publish.
post.summary: Attach a workbook to an experiment

post.description: Detaches the current workbook from the experiment. The workbook version reference is kept for historical purposes.
post.summary: Detach the workbook from an experiment

post.description: Publishes the latest workbook cells as a new version (or reuses the latest if unchanged) and updates the experiment to reference it.
post.summary: Upgrade the experiment to the latest workbook version

post.description: Uploads tabular or structured data (CSV, TSV, parquet, JSON, NDJSON) into a user-defined table in the experiment. Either creates a new upload table or appends to an existing one.
post.summary: Upload generic tabular or structured data

uploadTableId.description: Stable UUID of the logical upload table (assigned on first upload). Present for target-backed uploads.

uploadTableName.description: User-chosen display name for the upload table. Present for target-backed uploads.

runId.description: Databricks job run id for status polling

get.description: Returns the merged upload history for an experiment: completed uploads from the Delta history table plus active and failed runs from the Databricks job-runs API. Optionally filtered by upload table name.

post.description: Initiates an asynchronous export job for experiment table data

tableName.description: Name of the table to export

anonymizeContributors.description: Per-export override of the experiment's anonymise-contributors setting. Falls back to the experiment's stored value when omitted.

get.description: Lists all export jobs for an experiment table
Downloads a completed export file
Returns all locations associated with the specified experiment.

post.description: Associates one or more locations with an experiment.

put.description: Replaces all locations associated with an experiment.

get.description: Search for places using text query through AWS Location Service.
Get place information for given coordinates through AWS Location Service.
Retrieves a list of visualizations for a specific experiment

post.description: Creates a new visualization for an experiment with the provided configuration

get.description: Retrieves a specific visualization by its ID

patch.description: Updates an existing visualization with the provided data

get.description: Retrieves a list of dashboards for a specific experiment

post.description: Creates a new dashboard for an experiment

get.description: Retrieves a specific dashboard by its ID

patch.description: Updates an existing dashboard. When `widgets` is supplied, the array fully replaces the existing widgets.

post.summary: Add multiple annotations to experiment data

get.summary: List all transfer requests for the authenticated user

post.description: Creates experiment, command, macro, and optionally flow in a single atomic operation as part of a project transfer from an external platform

get.description: Retrieves all metadata records for an experiment

post.description: Creates a new metadata record for an experiment. Multiple metadata records can be attached to a single experiment.

put.description: Replaces the metadata blob on an existing metadata record. The entire metadata object is replaced.

delete.description: Removes a specific metadata record from an experiment

get.description: Returns the current server UTC timestamp for time synchronization.
Returns temporary AWS credentials for authenticated users to connect to AWS IoT Core. Credentials are valid for 15 minutes. Requires active Better Auth session.

accessKeyId.description: AWS Access Key ID for temporary credentials

secretAccessKey.description: AWS Secret Access Key for temporary credentials

sessionToken.description: AWS Session Token for temporary credentials

expiration.description: ISO 8601 date string when credentials expire

post.description: Returns a pre-signed S3 PutObject URL for uploading IoT payloads larger than 128 KB. The URL is valid for 15 minutes. Upload the JSON payload directly to the returned URL using HTTP PUT.
post.summary: Get pre-signed S3 upload URL for large IoT payloads

experimentId.description: UUID of the experiment to associate this upload with

uploadUrl.description: Pre-signed S3 PutObject URL (valid for 15 minutes)

key.description: S3 object key where the payload will be stored

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 date string when the upload URL expires

get.description: Retrieves the IoT devices owned by the authenticated user

post.description: Registers a device as an AWS IoT Thing owned by the authenticated user. No certificate is issued at this stage.

serialNumber.description: Physical device identifier, e.g. MAC address

deviceType.description: IotDevice class, maps to the ingest topic sensorType

get.description: Retrieves a specific device owned by the authenticated user

delete.description: Deletes the device registration and its AWS IoT Thing

post.description: Issues an X.509 certificate for a pending device, attaches it to the Thing, and activates the device. Returns the certificate PEM and private key once; they are never retrievable again.

delete.description: Revokes the device certificate and marks the device as revoked. The device can no longer connect.

post.description: Issues a new certificate for an active device, retires the previous one, and returns the new certificate PEM and private key once.

get.description: Returns a list of macros based on the specified filter criteria

post.description: Creates a new macro with the given properties and processes the code file through Databricks

get.description: Returns a macro by its ID

put.description: Updates an existing macro and optionally processes a new code file through Databricks

delete.description: Deletes a macro by its ID

get.description: Returns commands that are marked as compatible with this macro
get.summary: List compatible commands for a macro

post.description: Links commands as compatible with this macro (creator only)
post.summary: Add compatible commands to a macro

delete.description: Unlinks a command from this macro's compatibility list (creator only)
delete.summary: Remove a compatible command from a macro

post.description: Executes a single macro against one measurement / data point and returns the result
Accepts a batch of items with macro_ids from Databricks pipelines, groups by macro, fetches scripts, invokes Lambda functions, and returns processed results

get.description: Returns a list of commands based on the specified filter criteria

post.description: Creates a new command with the given properties

get.description: Returns a command by its ID

patch.description: Updates a command with the given properties

delete.description: Deletes a command by its ID

get.description: Returns macros that are marked as compatible with this command
get.summary: List compatible macros for a command

post.description: Links macros as compatible with this command (creator only)
post.summary: Add compatible macros to a command

delete.description: Unlinks a macro from this command's compatibility list (creator only)
delete.summary: Remove a compatible macro from a command

get.description: Search for users by name or email with pagination support

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query for name or email

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of users to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of users to skip for pagination

get.description: Returns a single user by their unique identifier

delete.description: Deletes a user by their ID if allowed

post.description: Creates user profile and sets the user as registered

activated.description: Whether the profile is active or deactivated

avatarUrl.description: Avatar URL seeded from OAuth provider

get.description: Returns the user's profile information including firstName, lastName, bio, and organization
Returns the experiments where the user is the only admin (blocking account deletion), each with its other members as transfer candidates

post.description: Fetches user profile metadata (firstName, lastName, avatarUrl) for multiple user IDs to populate Databricks pipeline tables
post.summary: Get user metadata for Databricks pipelines

post.description: Creates an invitation for a user to join a resource.

get.description: Returns all pending invitations for a given resource.

patch.description: Updates the role on a pending invitation.

role.description: New role to assign to the invitation

delete.description: Revokes a pending invitation so it can no longer be accepted.

get.description: Returns the timestamp the authenticated user last opened the What's new panel, used to compute the unread indicator. null means the user has never opened it.
get.summary: Get the caller's What's new last-seen timestamp

lastSeenAt.description: ISO timestamp the user last opened the What's new panel; null = never seen

post.description: Sets the authenticated user's What's new last-seen timestamp to now, clearing the unread indicator across their devices. Returns the new timestamp.

lastSeenAt.description: ISO timestamp the user last opened the What's new panel; null = never seen

get.description: Returns a list of workbooks based on the specified filter criteria

post.description: Creates a new workbook with the given properties

get.description: Returns a workbook by its ID

delete.description: Deletes a workbook by its ID (owner only)

get.description: Returns all published versions for a workbook, ordered by version descending
Returns a specific workbook version with its full cell data
